response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior generalist with deep web, backend, and DLang experience, but the role demands native-level Japanese, C-on-UNIX embedded firmware expertise, Linux kernel modification, and LAN-switch security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, switch chip SDKs) — none of which appear on the resume. The skills mismatch is severe enough that hard requirements (Japanese, C, kernel work) are not met.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across diverse stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and DLang high-performance services
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language ability (hard requirement: native-level)
No embedded firmware, Linux kernel modification, or LAN-switch/networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)
Primary recent work is TypeScript/Node web platforms — C on UNIX is a brief, decade-old role
